Temporary crowns and sealing materials were removed, abutment teeth and restorations were cleaned, and the fit of the new restorations was checked. The fit was satisfactory, and the patient was pleased. Dual-cure resin cement was used for final cementation, occlusion was adjusted, and the restorations were polished.
Medical Instructions: Avoid biting hard objects, maintain oral hygiene, and return for follow-up as needed.

Medical Instructions: Avoid biting hard foods and maintain oral hygiene. Return for follow-up as needed.

The fit was satisfactory, and the patient was pleased. Dual-cure resin cement was used for final cementation, occlusion was adjusted, and the restorations were polished.
Medical Instructions: Avoid biting hard objects, maintain oral hygiene, and return for follow-up as needed.

Patient Record: Initial consultation. Diagnosis: Dental caries (K02.901). Treatment: Resin restoration.
Examination: Teeth 36 and 46 exhibit carious lesions on the buccal and occlusal surfaces. Negative percussion and gingival tests, normal cold test response.
Radiographic Examination: No radiographs taken.
Diagnosis: Dental caries on teeth 36 and 46 (K02.901).
Treatment Plan: Resin restoration for teeth 36 and 46.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: Caries removal and cavity preparation were performed on teeth 36 and 46. Acid etching, rinsing, and drying were followed by bonding agent application, thinning, and light curing. Resin was placed in layers, each layer light-cured, followed by shaping, occlusal adjustment, and polishing.
Medical Instructions: Avoid biting hard objects. Return for follow-up if any discomfort arises.
Remarks:

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Moderate caries in permanent teeth (K02.800x007). Treatment: Veneer restoration.
Examination: Teeth 11 and 21 have proximal caries extending to the middle layer of dentin, negative to probing, cold, heat, and percussion tests.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Moderate caries in permanent teeth (K02.800x007) on teeth 11 and 21.
Treatment Plan: Veneer restoration for teeth 11 and 21.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For teeth 11 and 21, the patient was informed about the condition, treatment plan, costs, and prognosis, and consent was obtained. Caries removal on labial and proximal surfaces was performed, followed by cavity preparation, etching, drying, resin filling, veneer preparation, finishing, polishing, and impression taking with silicone for the upper arch and alginate for the lower arch. The restoration will be fabricated and fitted at a later date.
Medical Instructions: Avoid chewing on the treated side for 2 hours. Return for follow-up if symptoms occur. Avoid biting hard objects.

Treatment Recommendations: Regular scaling and dental check-ups.
Management: The patient was informed about the condition, treatment plan, costs, and prognosis, and consented to treatment. Medical Instructions: Avoid acidic foods and extreme temperatures for one week. Maintain oral hygiene with mouthwash. Schedule regular check-ups and return if discomfort occurs.

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Dental arch defect.
Examination: Tooth 11 is missing, with normal mucosa. Teeth 12 and 22 show no abnormalities.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Tooth 11: Dental arch defect.
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For teeth 12 and 21, the patient was informed about the pros and cons of various restorative materials, associated costs, treatment time, possible follow-up visits, and complications, and voluntarily chose a 3-unit all-ceramic crown restoration for teeth 12-21. Abutment preparation was done, fine-tuning, silicone rubber impression was taken, silicone rubber bite record was made, and temporary crown restoration was performed. Shade matching was done.
Medical Instructions: Return if discomfort occurs. Avoid biting excessively hard foods, maintain oral hygiene, and attend follow-up appointments as scheduled.

Medical Instructions: Maintain oral hygiene and attend scheduled follow-ups.

Examination: Healing abutments are in place on teeth 12 and 21. The gingiva in the implant area shows no significant redness or swelling.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Teeth 11, 12, and 21: Partial edentulism (K08.100x002).
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: Healing caps were removed from teeth 12 and 21. The area was rinsed and dried. Impression posts were placed, and an open-tray polyether impression was taken. Healing caps were reinserted. Occlusal records and shade selection were completed.
Medical Instructions: Maintain oral hygiene and attend scheduled follow-ups.

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: dental arch defect (K08.100x002).
Examination: Tooth 36 implant crown is stable, with buccal soft tissue redness and swelling, pus discharge upon pressure, bleeding on probing, and food debris in the gingival pocket.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Tooth 36, dental arch defect (K08.100x002).
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: The implant crown on tooth 36 was removed, the area was irrigated, medication was applied, and a healing abutment was placed.
Medical Instructions: Return if discomfort occurs. Avoid biting hard objects and schedule a follow-up in two weeks.
Remarks:

Examination: Tooth 36 shows grade II mobility, gingival redness and swelling, bleeding on probing, and periodontal pockets.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Periodontitis.
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For tooth 36, the patient was informed about the condition, treatment plan, costs, and prognosis, and consented to treatment. Local infiltration anesthesia with 4% Articaine was administered, followed by subgingival scaling, root planing, and laser treatment. The area was irrigated with hydrogen peroxide and saline, and a steam eye mask was applied. Oral hygiene instruction (OHI) was provided, including brushing, flossing, and interdental brush use.
Medical Instructions: Avoid acidic foods and extreme temperatures for one week. Maintain oral hygiene with mouthwash. Schedule regular check-ups and return if discomfort occurs.

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Tooth structure defect (K07.303). Treatment: Resin restoration.
Examination: Tooth 23 is temporarily sealed with no soft tissue abnormalities.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Tooth 23: Tooth structure defect (K07.303).
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For tooth 23, the temporary seal was removed, resin filling was performed, routine tooth preparation, fine finishing, and polishing were done. 3Shape scanning of the upper and lower arches was conducted to transfer the occlusal relationship. A restoration was fabricated, and a temporary crown was placed, with occlusion adjusted and polished.
Medical Instructions: Avoid biting hard objects, maintain oral hygiene, and follow up as needed.

Medical Instructions: Avoid biting hard objects, maintain oral hygiene, and return for follow-up.

Examination: Teeth 11 and 12 have temporary crowns in place, with no abnormalities in the surrounding soft tissue.

The fit was satisfactory, and the patient was pleased. The restorations were cemented with dual-cure resin cement, occlusion was adjusted, and the restorations were polished.
Medical Instructions: Avoid biting hard objects. Maintain oral hygiene. Return for follow-up as needed.

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Deep caries.
Examination: Teeth 11, 12, 21, and 22 show brown discoloration at the contact points, with soft probing surfaces and no mobility. Percussion test is negative.
Radiographic Examination: Teeth 11, 12, 21, and 22 show caries approaching the pulp.
Diagnosis: Deep caries in teeth 11, 12, 21, and 22.
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: Veneers and crowns for teeth 11, 12, 21, and 22.
Management: Alginate impressions were taken for both arches to transfer occlusal relationships. Diagnostic wax-up was created, and intraoral photographs were taken. Tooth preparation was scheduled for a later date.
Medical Instructions: Return if any discomfort arises.
Remarks:

Examination: Teeth 31, 32, 33, 41, 42, and 43 retain temporary crowns, with no abnormalities in soft tissues.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Tooth structure loss in teeth 31, 32, 33, 41, 42, and 43 (K07.303).
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For teeth 31, 32, 33, 41, 42, and 43, the patient was informed of the condition, treatment plan, costs, and prognosis, and consented to treatment. Temporary crowns and sealing materials were removed, abutments and restorations cleaned, and restorations tried in for fit. The fit was satisfactory, and the patient was pleased. Dual-cure resin cement was used for permanent cementation, with occlusion adjustment and polishing.
Medical Instructions: Avoid biting hard objects. Maintain oral hygiene and return for follow-up.

ground_truth:
Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Moderate caries in permanent teeth (K02.800x007). Treatment: Filling.
Examination: Teeth 32, 33, 34, 41, 42, 43, and 44 have temporary veneers. Tooth 46 shows buccal caries extending to mid-dentin, with normal cold and heat tests.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Moderate caries in tooth 46 (K02.800x007).
Treatment Plan: Filling for tooth 46.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For teeth 32, 33, 34, 41, 42, 43, and 44, temporary veneers and provisional materials were removed, the abutments and restorations were cleaned, and the permanent restorations were tried in, showing good marginal fit and contact. Occlusion was adjusted, polished, and permanently cemented. For tooth 46, caries was removed to mid-dentin without pulp exposure, shade matching was done, gingival retraction was performed, and a proximal restoration system was used. Pulp protection, base lining, and layered composite filling were performed to restore occlusal morphology, followed by occlusal adjustment and polishing.
Medical Instructions: Avoid biting hard foods. Return if discomfort occurs.
Remarks:

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Pulpitis (K04.000).
Examination: Tooth 35 shows no percussion pain or gingival inflammation and is temporarily sealed.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Pulpitis in tooth 35 (K04.000).
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For tooth 35, the temporary seal was removed, post space was prepared, and a fiber post was bonded. The abutment tooth was prepared and refined. An intraoral scan and bite registration were taken, and a temporary crown was placed.
Medical Instructions: Avoid biting hard foods. Return for evaluation if discomfort occurs.
Remarks:

ground_truth:
Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Moderate caries in permanent tooth (K02.800x007). Treatment: Restoration.
Examination: Tooth 36 exhibits occlusal caries extending to the middle layer of dentin. Negative responses to probing, cold, heat, and percussion tests.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Moderate caries in permanent tooth 36 (K02.800x007).
Treatment Plan: Restoration.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: The patient was informed about the condition, treatment plan, costs, and prognosis, and consented to the procedure. A rubber dam was placed, caries were removed, and the cavity was prepared. The cavity was cleaned and dried, and a calcium hydroxide liner was placed near the pulp. Acid etching was performed, followed by rinsing and drying. A bonding agent was applied, thinned, and light-cured. Composite resin was layered and light-cured, followed by shaping, occlusal adjustment, and polishing.
Medical Instructions: Avoid chewing on the affected side for 2 hours. Return for follow-up if discomfort occurs. Avoid biting hard objects.
Remarks:

Patient Record: Initial consultation. Diagnosis: Deep caries. Treatment: Restoration.
Examination: Tooth 36 shows dark discoloration with a soft, hard texture on the buccal side, extending to the dentin layer. The tooth is sensitive to cold stimuli.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Deep caries on tooth 36.
Treatment Plan: Restoration for tooth 36.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: The patient was informed about the condition, treatment plan, costs, and prognosis, and consented to treatment. Caries removal was performed, followed by cavity preparation, acid etching, drying, bonding agent application, and layered composite filling. Occlusion was adjusted and polished.
Medical Instructions: Avoid biting hard foods. If pulpal symptoms occur, root canal treatment may be necessary. Return for follow-up if discomfort occurs.
Remarks: <end_of_turn>.
